domain: Pilkington float glass tin-bath leveling (1952): molten glass poured on molten tin bath; gravity + surface tension produce flat parallel both-sided sheets without mechanical pressing; substrate IS the leveler; N2/H2 protective atmosphere.
specific_mechanism: Passive leveling by floating on denser fluid substrate; hydrostatic equilibrium of substrate defines a flat reference plane; workpiece flatness emerges from floating, not from active force.
llm_application: FLOAT-GLASS tin-bath null-space activation flattening: at calibration time, compute per-layer reference manifold M_l = top-k principal directions of activation a_l on a calibration corpus (head-wise PCA). At inference, for each layer compute a_l_flat = Proj_M_l(a_l) + α * (a_l - Proj_M_l(a_l)) with α=0.5 (passive attenuation of null-space-relative-to-M_l component). Apply per-layer per-token, inference-only, no retraining. Differs from (a) FLAT-LLM (compresses weights via activation-space PCA training-free at deployment; ours is INFERENCE-TIME attenuation, not weight compression), (b) singular-value pruning (one-shot weight removal), (c) FlatQuant (flatness for quantization), (d) AWQ activation-aware (quantization weights not inference attenuation) by combining (i) FIXED calibration-time PCA reference + (ii) INFERENCE-TIME attenuation + (iii) α-PARAMETERIZED null-space damping.
novelty_claim: FLAT-LLM uses activation PCA reference for weight compression. AWQ uses activation outliers for quantization. SmoothQuant rescales weight+activation for quantization. FLOAT-GLASS recombines (a) ACTIVATION PCA REFERENCE + (b) INFERENCE-TIME (not weight-time) APPLICATION + (c) α-DAMPED null-space attenuation. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
